slides.title: Python Comes Pre-Stocked
slides.body: Python ships with a massive built-in toolkit called the **standard library** — hundreds of ready-to-use modules for math, dates, files, JSON, and more. No installation needed.
slides.tip: This is what "batteries included" means — Python brings the power, you just plug it in.

slides.title: One Line to Unlock a Module
slides.body: Use `import module_name` to load any standard library module. Then access its tools with a dot: `module.function()`.
slides.tip: You only need to import once per file — Python remembers it for the whole script.

slides.title: random & datetime Are Your Friends
slides.body: `random` picks numbers and makes choices. `datetime` handles dates so you never count days manually.
slides.code:
import random
from datetime import date

print(random.randint(1, 6))
print(date.today())
slides.tip: Use `from module import name` when you want to skip typing the module name each time.

slides.title: json & collections Save Real Work
slides.body: `json` converts between Python dicts and JSON text in one call. `collections.Counter` tallies items instantly — no loops needed.
slides.code:
from collections import Counter

words = ["apple", "banana", "apple"]
print(Counter(words))
slides.tip: Before writing a counting loop yourself, check if Counter already does it.

slides.title: Always Check stdlib First
slides.body: Before reaching for `pip install`, spend 30 seconds checking the standard library. It's free, always available, and works on any Python machine.
slides.tip: Run `dir(module)` to see everything a module contains, or search docs.python.org for the full index.
